title: Successful login returns valid auth token
notes: Baseline positive case. Capture token for use in all subsequent authenticated requests.

title: Login fails with incorrect password
notes: Negative case. Response should not reveal whether the email exists.

title: Login fails with unregistered email
notes: Negative case, same generic message as API-002 expected for security (no user enumeration).

title: Login fails with missing required fields
notes: Schema validation case: omits required 'password' field.

title: Access protected endpoint with valid x-auth-token succeeds
notes: Auth token validation: confirms a valid token grants access.

title: Access protected endpoint with missing auth token is rejected

expectedResponse.message: Access token is not valid or has expired, you will need to login

notes: Auth token validation - negative case, no x-auth-token header sent at all.

title: Access protected endpoint with malformed/garbage token is rejected

expectedResponse.message: Access token is not valid or has expired, you will need to login

notes: Auth token validation - negative case with syntactically invalid token.

title: Access protected endpoint with expired token is rejected

expectedResponse.message: Access token is not valid or has expired, you will need to login

notes: ASSUMPTION: token TTL not independently confirmed. Requires a token deliberately aged past expiry, or logout-then-reuse (see API-009), to obtain a genuinely expired token for this test.

title: Token is invalidated immediately after logout

expectedResponse.message: User has been successfully logged out

notes: Follow-up: reuse this same token on GET /notes and expect 401 (API-010).

title: Reusing a token after logout is rejected

expectedResponse.message: Access token is not valid or has expired, you will need to login

notes: Confirms server-side token invalidation, not just client-side session clearing.

title: Create a new note with valid data succeeds
notes: CRUD - Create. Verify returned 'id' is usable in subsequent GET/PUT/DELETE calls.

title: Create note fails when required 'title' field is missing
notes: Schema validation - required field omitted.

title: Create note fails with invalid category enum value
notes: ASSUMPTION: exact allowed enum values not independently confirmed from static docs — verify against live API's schema definitions before automating the exact message text.

title: Create note fails with wrong data type for 'completed' field
notes: Schema validation - type mismatch, string instead of boolean.

title: Create note fails when unauthenticated

expectedResponse.message: Access token is not valid or has expired, you will need to login

notes: Auth validation on a write operation, no x-auth-token sent.

title: Get all notes returns only the authenticated user's notes
notes: CRUD - Read (list). Cross-reference with a User B token to confirm no data leakage.

title: Get a single note by valid ID succeeds
notes: CRUD - Read (single). Use an ID from a note created in API-011 or seeded data.

title: Get a note by non-existent ID returns 404
notes: Error handling - well-formed but non-existent ID.

title: Get a note with a malformed ID returns 400

expectedResponse.message: Note ID 'not-a-valid-id' is not a valid ID

notes: Error handling - malformed ID format (not a valid ObjectId/UUID) should return 400, not 500.

title: Get a note belonging to another user is forbidden/not found
notes: ASSUMPTION: API may return 404 (hides existence) or 403 (confirms existence, denies access) — confirm actual behavior against live API; this is an important security-relevant detail to verify.

title: Update a note (PUT) with valid full payload succeeds
notes: CRUD - Update (full replace).

title: Update a note (PUT) fails with missing required field
notes: Schema validation on update, same as create.

title: Update completion status (PATCH) toggles 'completed' correctly
notes: CRUD - Update (partial). Maps to 'Sprint planning' style completion toggle from the dashboard.

title: PATCH note fails with invalid data type for 'completed'
notes: Schema validation - type mismatch on partial update.

title: Update a note that does not exist returns 404
notes: Error handling on update to a non-existent resource.

title: Delete a note with valid ID succeeds
notes: CRUD - Delete. Follow up with GET on same ID to confirm it now 404s (API-027).

title: Deleted note is no longer retrievable
notes: Confirms deletion actually persisted, not just a soft-hide in the UI layer.

title: Deleting a non-existent note returns 404
notes: Error handling - idempotency/negative case for delete.

title: Register a new user with valid data succeeds
notes: Use a dynamically generated email per run to avoid 'already registered' collisions across test executions.

title: Register fails with an already-registered email

expectedResponse.message: An account already exists with the same email address

notes: Error handling - conflict case.

title: Register fails with invalid email format

expectedResponse.message: A valid email address is required

notes: Schema validation - malformed email string.

title: Register fails with weak/short password

expectedResponse.message: Password must be between 6 and 30 characters

notes: ASSUMPTION: exact min/max length not independently confirmed — verify against live API before asserting exact message.

title: Malformed JSON body returns 400, not 500

expectedResponse.message: <parser/bad request error, exact text may vary>

notes: Error handling - malformed JSON at the transport level should be caught gracefully by the API, not surface a raw stack trace or 500.

title: SQL/NoSQL injection attempt in note fields is safely rejected or sanitized
notes: Security/negative case: input should be stored as literal text, not executed. Expect success with data stored verbatim and escaped safely, not a 500 or data corruption.

title: Health check endpoint confirms service is up
notes: No auth required. Useful as a pre-flight smoke check before running the full suite.

title: Exceeding rate limit returns 429 Too Many Requests

expectedResponse.message: Too many requests, please try again later.

notes: ASSUMPTION: rate-limiting threshold and exact response not confirmed from static docs. Test by firing N rapid consecutive requests (e.g. 100+ in a tight loop) and asserting a 429 appears; determine actual N empirically against the live API and record it here before finalizing.

title: Unsupported HTTP method on a valid endpoint returns 404/405

expectedResponse.message: <method not allowed or route not found>

notes: Error handling edge case - confirm the API returns a clean 4xx rather than crashing on an unexpected verb.

title: Requesting a completely invalid route returns 404
notes: General error handling for unmapped routes.

title: Change password with correct current password succeeds
notes: CRUD-adjacent user account operation. Follow up by confirming old password no longer authenticates.

title: Change password fails with incorrect current password
notes: Negative case for password change flow.
